analysis: engine speed bottleneck analysis vs Edax

endgameExactSolve.positions: FFO #40-44 (bench/ffo_positions.json, same positions as engine's existing FFO fast bench)

ours.source: existing cargo test -p engine --release --test ffo_bench run this session (not re-measured)

midgameFixedDepth12.note: The user's previously-known '~1.1us/node, ~890K NPS' figure comes from a different single representative position (T176 measurement, MPC on, unlimited time). Per-node cost is position-dependent; both are legitimate but not directly comparable without noting this.

componentMicrobenchmarks.method: release-build isolated hot-loop microbenchmarks (2,000,000 iterations each, std::hint::black_box to suppress dead-code elimination), added temporarily to engine/src/search.rs as #[ignore]d tests, measured, then fully reverted via git checkout (zero diff in the committed engine/src/search.rs)

leafFractionMeasurement.method: temporary atomic counter incremented at negascout's depth==0 branch (static_eval call site), compared against SearchResult.nodes; counter and instrumentation fully reverted after measurement (git checkout, zero diff)

perNodeCostModel.method: back-of-envelope model combining component microbenchmarks with call-count estimates derived from reading negascout/ordered_moves/etc_try_cutoff source; average branching factor assumed b=8
perNodeCostModel.conclusion: Neither evaluation (score(), ~15.5% of observed time) nor the measured board-manipulation components (movegen/flip/zobrist/TT, ~17.7% of observed time, including their repeated invocations from move-ordering and ETC) individually dominate. Combined they explain only ~33% of observed per-node time; the remaining ~67% is not decomposed by this pilot's microbenchmarks and is hypothesized to be recursive function-call overhead, aspiration/history bookkeeping, and cache/branch-prediction effects not reproduced by isolated hot-loop benchmarks.
perNodeCostModel.keyFinding: The zobrist_hash incremental primitive (incremental_move_hash) already exists and is tested but is unused in negascout's hot path; wiring it in could save an estimated ~8% of total per-node time at low cost/risk.

rows.edaxKnownApproach: incremental per-pattern-feature update on move application
rows.ourImplementation: PatternWeights::score() recomputes all 46 pattern instances from scratch every call, no incremental state
rows.gapSeverity: large, high implementation cost to close

rows.edaxKnownApproach: incremental XOR update on move application
rows.ourImplementation: incremental_move_hash exists and is tested but negascout calls the naive full-board-scan zobrist_hash() instead
rows.gapSeverity: small cost/risk to close, concrete near-term candidate

rows.ourImplementation: 8-direction shift loop (flips_for_move); per-call cost (38ns) itself is competitive, but call count is inflated by ordered_moves and etc_try_cutoff repeating it per candidate move
rows.gapSeverity: call-count problem more than per-call cost

rows.ourImplementation: 2-tier bucket structure; probe 5.67ns / store 23.82ns, not obviously slow in isolation

rows.edaxKnownApproach: iterative deepening with flexible time allocation
rows.ourImplementation: fixed-depth + node-budget/wall-insurance hybrid (T175/T176 depth-based pilot in progress)
rows.gapSeverity: structural, affects effective depth not raw NPS (T177 scope)

rows.ourImplementation: implemented and calibrated (T156-T176, t=1.0 selected), used in this comparison too
rows.gapSeverity: parity, comparison is broadly fair on this axis

optimizationPriorityList.candidate: Wire incremental Zobrist hash (incremental_move_hash) into negascout, eliminating both the node's own full-recompute and etc_try_cutoff's redundant per-candidate recompute
Simplify move-ordering heuristic (avoid full apply_move+opponent legal_moves per candidate)
optimizationPriorityList.risk: medium (could increase node count via worse ordering; requires A/B verification of net effect)

optimizationPriorityList.candidate: Incremental evaluation (score()), Edax-style: recompute only patterns touched by the move's flipped cells
optimizationPriorityList.expectedReductionPercentOfTotal: largest potential lever but not precisely bounded by this pilot
optimizationPriorityList.risk: medium-high (requires bit-exact verification machinery akin to T163's D4 canonicalization)
optimizationPriorityList.t177Relation: propose as a separate design-consult task, not bundled with T177

optimizationPriorityList.candidate: Proper sampling profiler (perf/VTune) to decompose the unexplained ~67% of per-node time
optimizationPriorityList.t177Relation: useful to run before T177 for better-informed design

optimizationPriorityList.expectedReductionPercentOfTotal: does not reduce NPS but improves effective search efficiency (addresses the '~10s per move' user complaint)

optimizationPriorityList.candidate: Investigate TT store cost (23.82ns, 4.2x probe cost)

measurementDiscipline.exclusiveWindowConfirmed: checked via PowerShell Get-Process for python/curl/aria2 processes before running Edax/microbenchmarks; none found (T181's download had completed and its conversion/training had not yet started a CPU-heavy process)
measurementDiscipline.revertMethod: git checkout -- engine/src/search.rs (full revert, zero diff against the pre-T180 committed state)
measurementDiscipline.testResultsAfterRevert: cargo test -p engine --lib: 243 passed, 0 failed, 2 ignored (unchanged from baseline)
